title: Sending emails through MY email instead of Gmail / Outlook
excerpt: I’m looking at the option to send an email to a lead through the dashboard. It’s pretty common that we’ll talk to someone and they ask us to email more information, so there’s value in building a template to send and the…

posts.author: GoNC (Jason Carlton)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m looking at the option to send an email to a lead through the dashboard. It’s pretty common that we’ll talk to someone and they ask us to email more information, so there’s value in building a template to send and then keep track of any replies.

But is there a way to send the email through my server, instead of Gmail or Outlook? I paid a lot of money for a 4-letter .COM, and even more on marketing it! LOL It’s going to look super unprofessional for my staff to email out from Gmail instead of the one we’re known for.

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml:
why aren’t you using google or microsoft to host your email for your custom domain already? I’m surprised you’re not using Microsoft Office Online for your business already.

Outlook and gmail are the integration names for the microsoft and google platforms, which you can use with your custom domain based accounts on those platforms.

posts.author: GoNC (Jason Carlton)
posts.cookedHtml:
Technically I do use Google to host my email. I have a Google One account, so on the server I have emails sent to me@mysite.com delivered to me@gmail.com.

Then in Gmail, I changed “Send mail as” to me@mysite.com, and it’s sent through mail.mysite.com. This way, recipients see that the email came from me, not Gmail. I can also track the sent emails from all employees in WHM, which is very helpful when emails aren’t being delivered!

The Monday dashboard let me connect to my Gmail, but the outgoing is me@gmail.com; not me@mysite.com.

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml: Ah, Google Workspace is what you need, in that situation your custom domain becomes the actual domain of the google worksprace. Its not just mail.mysite.com forwarding to gmail. Your mail.mysite.com goes away entirely, with Google Workspace taking on that role. In that circumstance you’d be able to login to the gmail integration using your email@mysite.com address, and it would send and receive through it directly.

posts.author: dharmikjagodana (DHARMIK JAGODANA)
posts.cookedHtml:
With EasyMails, you have the flexibility to use your own SMTP configuration to send emails.

This means you can maintain your professional image by sending emails directly from your own server, rather than relying on Gmail or Outlook. Additionally, EasyMails allows you to connect multiple sender accounts, giving you even more control over your email communications. Give it a try and see how it can enhance your email workflow!

posts.author: otismilburnn (Otis Milburnn)
posts.cookedHtml: Have you tried SMTP service provider like iDealSMTP, Mailgun, and SMTPget with your email provider directly in Monday.com? That way, you can send emails through your own email server instead of relying on Gmail or Outlook.

posts.author: harrybrook (Harry Brook)
posts.cookedHtml: To send emails through your own domain instead of Gmail or Outlook, configure SMTP settings in your email client or server. Ensure correct authentication and SMTP server details. For personalized assistance and reliable SMTP solutions, consider consulting Digitalaka ™. Their expertise can help streamline your email communication process effectively.

posts.author: Brayden212 (Brayden)
posts.cookedHtml:
• Enable professional email communication by enabling your server to send emails straight from your domain.
  • Consider purchasing SMTP Relay Service from Digitalaka™ to ensure flawless integration.
  • Configuring DNS records for SPF and DKIM improves email delivery and security. Create a branded email template to provide a consistent brand impression.
  • Integrate this configuration with your dashboard to improve workflow. Thoroughly test the system, monitor responses, and assure compliance with industry requirements.

posts.author: Jorge880 (Jorge baker)
posts.cookedHtml: Have you considered integrating SMTP service providers like iDealSMTP, Mailgun, or SMTPget directly into your Monday.com account? This allows you to send emails through your own server, bypassing the need for Gmail or Outlook, potentially enhancing control and customization of your email communications.

posts.author: bibujakera (Bibi Bujakera)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Jason,

Did you get a solution? I’m having the same issue.

Regards,
Bibi

posts.author: jsellers (Jonathan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Google workspace is just another tool. Why would yo uadvocate that one tool brand is “what you need”, when what is needed is the abillity to bypass “big box” email providers, and use the email client of your choice, and the domain that you already own. Your method just feeds Google private and confidential information for free. When google shows respect for privacy, and pays out for the value of your confidential information is when google can get a credible nod. Otherwise, they’re just PI collectors that use your information to better their product for free. You’re saying that google should just get the content of all your emails for free. In other words, there is nothing of value to you in your emails so just give it to google for a convenience within the Monday software.

Really you should encourage Monday to do a better job of their email solution.

posts.author: jsellers (Jonathan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Why aren’t you using your domain host to run your own emails? This way you can use Proton (or any other email client) to provide the email experience you’re looking for. Microsoft is a never ending parade of changes, higher prices, and privacy concerns - not to mention the “big brother” component to both Google and Microsoft.

Technically competent software developers don’t cater to big brands, they cater to protocols and standards. Monday should demonstrate competency, and create the ability to manage emails through the domain of their customer’s business. It looks amateur when sending emails through the domain of someone else. It builds credibility to the brand when emails are from the same domain as the website. Only amateurs insist on using Google/Microsoft exclusively. Technically competent software allows for full customization based on protocols that run the internet/email/websites/databases/etc.

posts.author: helpemailtales (Lyvera Solen)
posts.cookedHtml:
GoNC:

I’m looking at the option to send an email to a lead through the dashboard. It’s pretty common that we’ll talk to someone and they ask us to email more information, so there’s value in building a template to send and then keep track of any replies.

But is there a way to send the email through my server, instead of Gmail or Outlook? I paid a lot of money for a 4-letter .COM, and even more on marketing it! LOL It’s going to look super unprofessional for my staff to email out from Gmail instead of the one we’re known for.

Totally get this, sending from a Gmail or Outlook address can feel unprofessional when you’ve invested in your own domain. As far as I know, Monday currently requires Gmail or Outlook for sending emails from the platform, rather than a custom SMTP or mail server. Hopefully native support for custom domains is something they add, because it would be a big win for branding and consistency
